Co-authored-by: wuan <563314+wuan@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove pycache from git and add Python cache to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: wuan <563314+wuan@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix missing gcov library linker error by adding -lgcov to build_flags

PlatformIO's SCons build system for the native platform invokes the
compiler and linker as separate steps. The --coverage flag instruments
source code at compile time (generating __gcov_* calls), but when
PlatformIO calls the linker step it doesn't automatically add -lgcov
the way the g++ compiler driver would when called directly. This caused
"undefined reference to __gcov_init / __gcov_exit / __gcov_merge_add"
linker errors for all 4 test suites.

Adding -lgcov explicitly to build_flags ensures libgcov.a is included
during the link step, resolving all undefined gcov symbol errors.

Co-authored-by: wuan <563314+wuan@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix deprecated sonar.cfamily.build-wrapper-output property

Replace the deprecated -Dsonar.cfamily.build-wrapper-output with
-Dsonar.cfamily.compile-commands pointing to the compile_commands.json
file generated by build-wrapper inside its output directory, as
recommended by SonarCloud documentation.

Co-authored-by: wuan <563314+wuan@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: ARM 32 compatibilty fixes (#841)

* Fix ARM32 segfault in extension registration: use %ld for long instead of %lld

The %lld format specifier consumes 8 bytes but long is 4 bytes on ARM32,
causing subsequent varargs (hash pointer) to be misread and segfaulting in strlen().
Regression introduced in PR #633 when %d was changed to %lld without updating the
variable type from long to long long.

* Add Debian 11 ARM32 build job using QEMU emulation

Adds arm32-container-job that builds on ubuntu-latest with QEMU
ARM emulation via docker/setup-qemu-action. Runs the full build
pipeline (install-deps, build, test) inside a debian:11 arm/v7
Docker container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Prioritize dpkg for detecting userspace architecture

Update determine_machine_architecture function to prefer dpkg for userspace architecture detection.

* Replace CMake 3.20+ commands with CMake 3.5-compatible alternatives

Replace cmake_path() and file(COPY_FILE) calls with
get_filename_component() and configure_file(COPYONLY) respectively,
to match the project's cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5).

Also add out*/ to .gitignore to cover build output directories
with suffixes (e.g. out-debian11-arm32/).

* support armhf

* arm64

* remove arm32 buid

* fix: store flag values at word[0] for LE convention

Change GER_KNOWN_FLAG and IS_FAUCET_REGISTERED_FLAG to be stored at
word[0] instead of word[3]. Under LE, `push.0.0.0.FLAG` puts FLAG at
stack top = word[0], matching the documented layout [1, 0, 0, 0].

Previously `push.FLAG.0.0.0` placed FLAG at stack bottom = word[3],
resulting in Word([0, 0, 0, 1]) which contradicted the docs.

commit.message:
Fix crash in MonitorState refresh loop and UInt64 underflow

- Replace try? with try in Task.sleep so CancellationError propagates
  and exits the loop cleanly. Previously, if the task was cancelled
  (e.g. during shutdown), the error was silently swallowed and the
  loop would spin — firing refresh() at full speed with no delay,
  flooding the system with sysctl calls.

- Guard against UInt64 underflow in SystemMonitor.cpuUsage: if active
  ticks decreased (CPU offline, frequency scaling), the unsigned
  subtraction would wrap to a huge value. Now returns 0 in that case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(core): implement OSC 104 reset color palette

Add OSC 104 support to VTParser. OSC 104 resets one or more color
palette entries (as set via OSC 4) back to their defaults.

- No-argument form (`OSC 104 ST`) resets the entire palette; the
  registered callback is invoked once with index -1.
- Parameterised form (`OSC 104;c1;c2 ST`) resets each listed index;
  the callback is invoked once per index with values 0-255.

Implementation:
- Added `onOsc104` private field and `setOsc104Callback()` method.
- Extended `oscDispatch()`: the no-semicolon early-return path now
  checks for OSC 104 before returning; a new `case 104` in the switch
  handles the parameterised form.

Tests: 7 new tests in parser.test.ts covering BEL and ST terminators,
single index, multiple indices, boundary values (0/255), and the
no-callback no-throw guarantee.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: make streamable-http transport reachable through Cloudflare tunnel

   Three chained bugs prevented the MCP server from being accessible via
   https://agent-trust.radi.pro/mcp:

   1. Server bound to 127.0.0.1 inside the container — Docker cannot
      port-forward to container-localhost. Fixed by adding ENVIRONMENT=production
      to docker-compose.yml, which makes server.py bind uvicorn to 0.0.0.0.

   2. Production mode crashed on startup requiring TLS_CERT_PATH/TLS_KEY_PATH —
      but FastMCP has never supported ssl_certfile/ssl_keyfile, so TLS must
      always be terminated by the reverse proxy. Removed the sys.exit(1) and
      replaced it with an informational log.

   3. FastMCP's DNS rebinding protection rejected Host: host.docker.internal —
      the header cloudflared sends when forwarding requests to the origin.
      Disabled the protection on the FastMCP instance; Cloudflare handles
      host validation at the edge.

   Also fix the docker-compose port binding from 127.0.0.1:8140 to 0.0.0.0:8140
   so cloudflared (on a separate Docker network) can reach the host port via
   the Docker bridge gateway (172.17.0.1).

   Remove dead tls_cert_path/tls_key_path config fields and document the
   ENVIRONMENT variable in README and CLAUDE.md.

   Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: net_r_realized includes full trade outcome + engine-specific PTP params

Two bugs in simulate_trade_outcome():

1. net_r_realized = realized_r (PTP portion only)
   Should be total_r (PTP + remaining position result)
   Effect: losses showed -0.05R (commission only) instead of -1.05R
           wins showed 0.45R (PTP) instead of actual total outcome
   Fix: replace round(realized_r,3) -> round(total_r,3) on all exit paths

2. PTP params hardcoded to engine_params['judas_swing']
   Should use signal.engine_name to get correct engine's partial_tp_rr/vol
   Effect: ict_enigma (ptp_rr=1.75) and ict_venom (ptp_rr=1.0) both used
           judas_swing defaults instead of their optimizer-tuned values
   Fix: ep = settings.engine_params.get(signal.engine_name, {})

fix: drop unique constraint on module positions to fix resync errors

Replace the UNIQUE index idx_modules_path_position with a regular index.
The unique constraint caused failures during resync when modules had
duplicate positions from prior bugs. Position ordering is enforced by
the syncer, not the database.

commit.message:
fix: reset module positions before upsert to avoid unique constraint violation

When modules are reordered in content, the upsert could fail because the
new position conflicts with an existing module's position (idx_modules_path_position).
Fix by setting all positions to negative values before upserting.
